So the mechanic is:
- Click white diamond (w) → it becomes WHITE (W), purple square moves down
- Click purple square (p/M) → it moves down
Both actions move the purple square, but clicking white diamonds also converts them to WHITE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== Current path objects ===")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ('p', 'M', 'G', 'W', 'w') and node['pixels'] > 1:
        center_r = sum(p[0] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
        center_c = sum(p[1] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
        print(f"  {node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} center≈({center_r:.0f},{center_c:.0f})")

# Find all white diamonds (w) along path
w_diamonds = []
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'w' and node['pixels'] <= 5:
        center_r = sum(p[0] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
        if center_r > 25:
            w_diamonds.append((center_r, node['boundary'][0]))

w_diamonds.sort()
print(f"\nWhite diamonds to convert: {len(w_diamonds)}")
for r, pos in w_diamonds:
    print(f"  row≈{r:.0f} at {pos}")

# Click all white diamonds to convert them
print(f"\n=== Converting all {len(w_diamonds)} white diamonds ===")
for r, pos in w_diamonds:
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': pos[0], 'col': pos[1]}])
    print(f"  Click {pos}: done={result.get('done')}")
    if result.get('done'):
        print("  LEVEL COMPLETE!")
        break
    
    # Check purple square position
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for node in seg['nodes']:
        if node['color'] == 'p' and node['pixels'] > 10:
            center_r = sum(p[0] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
            center_c = sum(p[1] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
            print(f"    Purple square now at ({center_r:.0f},{center_c:.0f})")
            break

# After converting all, check if purple square is at endpoint
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\n=== Final state ===")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ('p', 'M', 'G', 'W', 'w') and node['pixels'] > 1:
        center_r = sum(p[0] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
        center_c = sum(p[1] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
        print(f"  {node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} center≈({center_r:.0f},{center_c:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
